excerpts.text: If I had a thousand pounds China should have it — if I had a thousand lives, China should have them. No! Not China, but Christ. Can we do too much for Him?
excerpts.source_title: Journal entry, cited in Hudson Taylor's Spiritual Secret

excerpts.text: The Great Commission is not an option to be considered; it is a command to be obeyed.
God's work done in God's way will never lack God's supply.
I am taking my shoes off, for the place on which I stand is holy ground. I do not think I shall ever need to put them on again.
excerpts.source_title: Letter to his mother, after arriving in China

excerpts.text: I have found that there are three stages in every great work of God: first, it is impossible, then it is difficult, then it is done.
The sun has never risen upon China without finding me at prayer.
excerpts.source_title: Personal testimony, cited in Taylor biographies

excerpts.text: I adopted Chinese dress, shaved my head except for a queue, and dyed it black, that I might the more readily gain access to the people.
How many there are in the church who, if confronted with the millions of China's perishing, would answer, 'Am I my brother's keeper?'
Do not have your concert first, and then tune your instrument afterwards. Begin the day with the Word of God and prayer, and get first of all into harmony with Him.

On Sunday, June 25th, 1865, unable to bear the sight of a congregation of a thousand or more Christian people rejoicing in their own security while millions perished, I wandered out on the sands alone, in great spiritual agony.
excerpts.source_title: Retrospect, cited in Hudson Taylor's Spiritual Secret

excerpts.text: There I prayed for twenty-four willing, skillful laborers, two for each of the eleven provinces of inland China which were without a missionary, and two for Mongolia.
It will not do to say that you have no special call to go to China. With these facts before you, you need rather to ascertain whether you have a special call to stay at home.
The China Inland Mission makes no appeals for money. We depend entirely on God. He is able to supply all our needs.
I felt that God had called me to China. There was nothing else I could do; I must obey.
When I cannot read, when I cannot think, when I cannot even pray, I can trust.
My dear wife, the light of my eyes and the joy of my heart, has been taken from me. But God is sovereign, and I bow before His will.
We wish to place a Christian missionary in every town and city of the Chinese Empire. We have no plan but God's plan; we ask for no money save from God.
The only persons to whom the Chinese ever willingly listen are those who have shown by their dress, their food, and their manner of life that they are in earnest.
I exchanged the good health and prospects of a medical career for a life of privation, danger, and unceasing toil. But I never looked back.
Ah, what it is to be far from home, in a land where every face is strange! But He who has called me will sustain me.
The Inland China, which has hitherto been entirely neglected, contains a population of some 180 million souls, without a single Protestant missionary.
I am no better than a servant. I must go where my Master sends me and do what He commands, not counting the cost.
All God's giants have been weak men who did great things for God because they reckoned on His being with them.
Want of trust is at the root of almost all our sins and all our weaknesses.
I am dying — no — I am going Home. The Chinese church will carry on. God's work does not depend on any one man.
excerpts.source_title: Last words, recorded by Howard Taylor
